observations: B3/non-goal note (NON-BLOCKING): tools/validate.sh's main() target-switch fall-through `die` message was updated from "neither .md nor .json" to "neither .md, .json, nor .html". The contract's non_goals[1] and scope purpose say 'no edit to any die()/require_* call ... strictly additive'. This one edit is the accurate, minimal, and forced corollary of the contractually-MANDATED new `*.html` target case (scope.files_modify[0].purpose + design_notes.three_mode_pinning explicitly require adding a `*.html)` branch to that same case statement); leaving the message unchanged would ship a stale extension enumeration -- itself a single-source-of-truth inconsistency, the exact defect class C10 exists to catch. It changes no check logic, no dependency guard, and no fail-closed behavior; the die still fires identically for genuinely-unsupported extensions. Judged intrinsic to the mandated *.html case, not scope creep -- surfaced for orchestrator awareness.
B2 signature confirmed exact: `check_c10_consistency <file> [<file> ...]` (local files=("$@")), matching scope.files_modify[0].purpose verbatim.

Sub-check design fidelity confirmed against design_notes.c10_subcheck_design_and_normalization_finding REV3: sub-check(1) is the format-agnostic python3 NORMALIZE (re.sub(r'<[^>]*>',' ') then re.sub(r'\s+',' ').strip()) -> SEGMENT (re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+')) -> per-segment vlib_no_stale_payload pipeline (NO physical-line join; none of the rejected REV0/1/2 line-join designs reimplemented). sub-check(2) P3-scoped, both-labels-required, boot_line>fallback_line FAIL, defensively guarded with `|| true` + presence tests. sub-check(3) External Anchor Rule reading NIZAM.json framework.version via python3, meta + footer anchors. Frontmatter-strip awk duplicated inline (strictly additive, check_c9_path_resolution untouched).

verify_lib.sh composed (vlib_no_stale_payload) but NOT modified and NOT re-sourced redundantly (no new `source` line in the diff; it was already sourced by C9). build_shipped_md_set/build_payload_md_set/frontmatter_python/check_c9_path_resolution and all C1-C9 check logic unchanged.
